levels.title: Identification & Form
levels.miniLesson:
🎯 Level 1: A, An, The, or Zero Article

In English, articles tell the reader if a noun is specific or general.

  • A / An: Singular, countable nouns introduced for the first time. (She read an article.)
  • The: Specific nouns known to both the writer and reader. (She read the article you assigned.)
  • Zero Article (∅): Plural or uncountable nouns speaking generally. (∅ Research takes time.)

questionBank.question: The university built ___ new library for the science department.
questionBank.explanation: Use 'a' because 'library' is a singular countable noun mentioned for the first time.

questionBank.question: ___ information provided in the lecture was difficult to understand.
questionBank.explanation: Use 'The' because it refers to specific information from that specific lecture.

questionBank.question: Many international students find that studying ___ English takes years of practice.
questionBank.explanation: Languages do not take an article when spoken about generally.

levels.title: Controlled Practice (L1 Traps)
levels.miniLesson:
⚠️ Level 2: The Japanese L1 Trap

Because Japanese does not use articles, it is very common to accidentally use "the" when talking about general plural or uncountable nouns. Do not use 'the' for general statements!

❌ The international students often face the culture shock.

✅ ∅ International students often face ∅ culture shock.

questionBank.question: ___ global warming is a major concern for scientists today.
questionBank.explanation: 'Global warming' is an uncountable noun being discussed as a general concept.

questionBank.question: ___ computers in the main lab are currently being updated.
questionBank.explanation: Even though it is plural, we use 'The' because we are talking about specific computers (the ones in the main lab).

questionBank.question: In modern society, ___ smartphones have changed how we communicate.
questionBank.explanation: We are speaking generally about all smartphones, so we use the zero article.

levels.title: Application & Error Correction
levels.miniLesson:
📝 Level 3: Paragraph Context

In TOEFL and IELTS essays, articles help connect your sentences. When you introduce a new idea, use a/an. When you refer back to that same idea in the next sentence, switch to the.

questionBank.question: Read the sentence and find the error: 'The government proposed a new law. A law will reduce pollution.'
options: Change 'a new law' to 'the new law'
questionBank.explanation: Once the law is introduced in the first sentence, it becomes specific. The second sentence must use 'The'.

questionBank.question: Read the sentence: 'She has a degree in the biology from the University of Tokyo.' Which article is incorrect?
questionBank.explanation: Academic subjects like 'biology' do not take an article.

questionBank.question: Select the correct sentence:
options: The society needs to protect an environment.
Society needs to protect the environment.
A society needs to protect environment.
questionBank.correctAnswer: Society needs to protect the environment.
questionBank.explanation: 'Society' is general (no article), but 'the environment' is a unique, specific entity.

levels.title: IELTS/TOEFL Integration
levels.miniLesson:
📈 Level 4: Task 1 Data Articles

In IELTS Task 1, articles are strictly graded when describing data. Memorize these specific collocations:

  • A significant increase / A dramatic drop
  • The number of / The highest percentage
  • Reached a peak of / Remained at the same level

questionBank.question: Between 2010 and 2015, there was ___ sharp decline in oil prices.
questionBank.explanation: Use 'a' with singular adjective-noun combinations describing trends (a sharp decline, a steady rise).

questionBank.question: The chart illustrates ___ proportion of students who study abroad.
questionBank.explanation: We use 'the' before 'proportion' or 'percentage' when describing a specific segment of a whole.

questionBank.question: In 2020, the sales figures reached ___ peak of 50,000 units.
questionBank.explanation: The correct academic collocation for Task 1 is 'reached a peak of'.
